ENKI ART · Terms of Use
← Back to home

ENKI ART — Terms of UseClosed Beta

Version: Closed Beta 0.1 · Last updated: 27.06.2026

Enki is currently in closed beta. Access is limited and invite-based while the platform is being developed and tested with a small group. Features and listings may still change as the platform evolves. By using Enki during the closed beta, you agree to the terms below.

1. What Enki is

Enki is a marketplace where artists list encrypted prompts. Buyers pay per use to generate an image and receive the output only — never the underlying prompt, workflow, or settings.

Enki is operated as an independent project by Kevin Germin, based in Braunschweig, Germany. There is no company behind it yet; it is run by an individual. Questions: kgermin@tuta.io.

2. Closed beta — no guarantees

During the closed beta, Enki is provided “as is” and “as available.” As the platform is still in active development, there is no guarantee of uptime, availability, accuracy, results, or data retention, and features, listings, balances, or accounts may change as the beta progresses. Please don’t rely on Enki for anything critical during this phase.

3. Your wallet, your funds

Enki is non-custodial. You control your own wallet and keys. Enki never takes custody of your tokens and cannot move your funds. Blockchain transactions are final and irreversible — they cannot be reversed, refunded, or charged back. You are solely responsible for securing your wallet; lost keys or access cannot be recovered by us.

4. AI-generated content

All images produced through Enki are generated by AI and are marked as such. Outputs are created by a third-party AI model, and your use of them is subject to that model provider’s terms and to applicable law. Enki makes no promise that an output is original, unique, or protectable, and claims no ownership of outputs.

5. Prohibited use — read this one

You must not use Enki to generate, request, or list prompts intended to produce:

  • Sexual content involving minors, or any content that sexualizes minors, in any form;
  • Non-consensual intimate imagery, or realistic depictions of real, identifiable people without their consent (including deepfakes);
  • Content that is illegal, infringes someone’s intellectual property or likeness rights, or facilitates fraud, harassment, or serious harm.

We may remove any prompt, output, or account, and refuse service, at any time — especially for anything in this section. To report something, contact kgermin@tuta.io.

6. Prompts belong to the artists

Prompts are the confidential property and trade secrets of the artists who list them. Buying a generation does not grant access to the prompt, and gives no exclusive rights — others may generate similar images from the same or similar prompts. Artists may remove a prompt at any time; images already generated remain usable by the buyer who made them.

7. Limitation of liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law, Enki and its creator are not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential loss arising from use of the platform. Nothing here excludes liability that cannot legally be excluded (such as for intent, gross negligence, or personal injury).

8. Eligibility

You must be 14 or older to use Enki.

9. Changes

These terms may change as the project develops. The current version always lives here. Continued use after a change means you accept it.

Enki is in closed beta. Buyers get the output. Artists keep the secret.
